UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores personal information for a registered member of the MindFlow AI platform. Authentication details are managed externally.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for communication.

registrationDate.description: The timestamp when the user registered on the platform.

lastLoginDate.description: The timestamp of the user's last successful login.

Course.title: Course
Course.description: Represents an educational course available on the MindFlow AI platform, focusing on AI-related topics.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Course entity.

description.description: A brief description or overview of the course content.

thumbnailUrl.description: URL to the course's thumbnail image or cover.

instructorName.description: The name of the instructor teaching the course.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the course was created or added to the platform.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the course details were last updated.

Module.title: Module
Module.description: A logical section or unit within a Course, containing a series of lessons.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Module entity.

description.description: A brief description of the module's content.

order.description: The sequential order of the module within its parent course.

Lesson.title: Lesson
Lesson.description: An individual learning unit within a Module, providing specific content like videos, text, or code examples.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Lesson entity.

description.description: A brief summary of what the lesson covers.

contentUrl.description: URL to the primary content for the lesson (e.g., embedded video, external document).

lessonType.description: The type of content for the lesson (e.g., 'video', 'text', 'code', 'quiz').

durationMinutes.description: The estimated duration of the lesson in minutes.

order.description: The sequential order of the lesson within its parent module.

UserCourseProgress.title: UserCourseProgress
UserCourseProgress.description: Tracks a specific user's overall progress and completion status for a particular course.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserCourseProgress entity.

startedAt.description: The timestamp when the user started the course.

completedAt.description: The timestamp when the user completed the course (null if not completed).

isCompleted.description: Indicates whether the user has fully completed the course.

lastAccessedLessonId.description: Reference to Lesson, indicating the last lesson the user accessed in this course. (Relationship: Lesson 1:N UserCourseProgress)

UserLessonCompletion.title: UserLessonCompletion
UserLessonCompletion.description: Records the completion status of individual lessons by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserLessonCompletion entity.

completedAt.description: The timestamp when the user marked the lesson as completed.

isCompleted.description: Indicates whether the user has completed this specific lesson.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Access is restricted to the owner, where the document ID `{userId}` matches `request.auth.uid`.
Contains details of all educational courses available on the platform. Publicly readable by all authenticated members.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific course.

definition.description: Stores modules belonging to a specific course. Publicly readable by all authenticated members. Includes 'courseId' in path for hierarchical structure.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent course.

params.name: moduleId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific module within a course.

definition.description: Stores individual lessons belonging to a specific module. Publicly readable by all authenticated members. Includes 'courseId' and 'moduleId' in path for hierarchical structure.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent course.

params.name: moduleId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent module.

params.name: lessonId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific lesson within a module.

definition.description: Tracks a user's overall progress for a specific course. Only accessible by the owning user, where `{userId}` matches `request.auth.uid`. Includes 'userId' in path for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user to whom this progress belongs.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the course being tracked.

definition.description: Records a user's completion status for individual lessons. Only accessible by the owning user, where `{userId}` matches `request.auth.uid`. Includes 'userId' in path for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user to whom this lesson completion belongs.

params.name: lessonId
params.description: The unique identifier of the lesson being completed.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to provide a secure, scalable, and debuggable member area for MindFlow AI, focusing on user profiles, course content, and personalized progress tracking. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
- For user-specific data like `UserProfile`, `UserCourseProgress`, and `UserLessonCompletion`, the user's unique ID (`userId`) is embedded directly into the document path (e.g., `/users/{userId}/...`). This critical design choice eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules to check ownership. Rules can simply compare `request.auth.uid` with the `{userId}` wildcard in the path, ensuring atomic authorization checks and greatly simplifying rule logic. For example, a rule for `/users/{userId}/courseProgress/{courseId}` would simply be `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == userId;`.
- For public content entities (`Course`, `Module`, `Lesson`), access is defined as 'readable by all authenticated users'. Since the authorization requirement is global for authenticated users, there's no complex per-document ownership or membership to denormalize; the rule applies uniformly to the entire collection.

**Support for Query-as-a-Privilege (QAPs):**
- **User-Owned Data:** Collections like `/users/{userId}/courseProgress` and `/users/{userId}/lessonCompletions` are inherently QAP-friendly. A user can only list or query documents within their own `/users/{userId}` subtree, as the `userId` in the path must match `request.auth.uid`. This prevents unauthorized users from querying or listing other users' private data.
- **Public Content:** Collections like `/courses`, `/courses/{courseId}/modules`, and `/courses/{courseId}/modules/{moduleId}/lessons` are designed for broad access. All authenticated users can list and read these documents. Since all documents in these collections share the same security posture (publicly readable by members), list operations are secure and don't require complex filtering in rules. This adheres to the 'structural segregation' principle, where data with different access needs is separated into distinct collections or subcollections.

**Overall Design Principles Adherence:**
- **Structural Segregation:** Public courses are segregated from private user progress data, ensuring distinct security postures per collection.
- **Access Modeling:** Path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}`) is used for private user data, and hierarchical paths are consistently applied for 1:N relationships (e.g., `Course 1:N Module 1:N Lesson`).
- **Data Clarity:** Wildcards are semantically descriptive (`userId`, `courseId`, `moduleId`, `lessonId`), and the schema is predictable.
